questions.text: What is the explicit, one-sentence intent of this scaffold? Is the goal to provide a reusable starting template for all future Go services, or is this a specific standalone service?
questions.context: The Intent section must be clearly and formally articulated. Understanding whether this is a reusable template vs. a one-off service changes the scope and design significantly.
options: Reusable scaffold/template for all future Go microservices in this repo/org
A specific standalone Go service that happens to start minimal
Both — a working service that also serves as a reference implementation

questions.text: Which specific Kubernetes health/uptime endpoints should the service expose?
questions.context: Kubernetes probes require specific routes. The PRD says 'typical' endpoints but does not enumerate them, which is insufficient for code generation.

questions.text: Which Echo middleware should be included in the default middleware stack?
questions.context: The PRD mentions 'typical handlers for logging, error handling and security' without specifying which ones. This is needed to generate the correct scaffolding code.

questions.text: What are the explicit Non-Goals for this scaffold? What should it deliberately NOT include?
questions.context: Non-Goals prevent scope creep and clarify what artifact generators should omit. For example: no auth, no database, no deployment YAML, no business logic.

questions.text: What is the background or motivation for creating this scaffold? Is there an existing service or template it replaces, or is this greenfield?
questions.context: The Background section provides context for why this work is being done. This helps reviewers and future maintainers understand the rationale.
options: Replacing an existing template or boilerplate
Standardizing across multiple teams/repos that currently diverge

questions.text: What Go module path and Go version should be used for this service?
questions.context: Code generation requires a concrete module name (e.g., github.com/org/skafolding) and a target Go version (e.g., 1.22).

questions.text: Should the scaffold include configuration management (e.g., environment variable parsing, a config struct)? If so, which library, if any, is preferred?
questions.context: Most production Go services need some form of config loading. It is unclear if this is in or out of scope for the initial scaffold.
options: Yes — env vars only (e.g., os.Getenv or godotenv)
Yes — structured config with a library (e.g., viper, envconfig, koanf)
No — keep it out of scope for now
